grammar_points.explain: get sb to do sth 属于 S+V+O+C 句型。get 是及物动词，them 是宾语，to act well for us 是宾语补足语，补充说明让它们做什么。注意：get 在这里不是“得到”，而是“使、让”。

grammar_points.name: 介词短语作状语
grammar_points.explain: For our own safety 是介词短语，放在句首作目的状语，说明主句动作的目的。in the right direction 也是介词短语，作地点状语，修饰 point。介词短语在句中常充当状语，表示时间、地点、原因、目的等。

aids.derivation: safe（安全的）+ -ty（名词后缀）→ 安全

synonyms.note: 更正式，常用于国家安全、网络安全等语境

aids.derivation: think（思考）+ -er（人）→ 思考者

synonyms.note: 同义词，但 thinker 更常见

aids.derivation: direct（指向）+ -ion（名词）→ 方向

synonyms.note: 更强调指导、引导，常用于抽象方向

expressions.usage: 写作中可用于表示引导某人做正确的事：The teacher pointed the students in the right direction.
可迁移到写作中表示“为某人利益行事”：We need AI to act well for humanity.

sentences.en: It is not yet clear whether this is possible, but if it is, it will require a cooperative spirit, and a willingness to set aside self-interest.
sentences.zh: 目前尚不清楚这是否可行，但如果可行，它将需要一种合作精神，以及一种愿意搁置私利的意愿。

grammar.type: it 形式主语 + whether 主语从句 + if 条件 + 并列宾语
grammar.note: 前半 It is not yet clear whether this is possible，it 为形式主语，whether... 为主语从句；后半 but if it is（省略 possible），it will require a cooperative spirit, and a willingness to set aside self-interest，两个宾语并列，to set aside self-interest 为不定式定语。

pattern.label: S + V + C(主语 + 系动词 + 补语)
pattern.plain: 去掉修饰和从句后，前半句主干是 It is clear(它是清楚的)，但加了 not yet 否定和时间，且真正主语是后面 whether 从句。后半句主干是 it will require a cooperative spirit and a willingness(它将需要合作精神和意愿)。

chunks.note: it 是形式主语，真正的主语是后面的 whether 从句。
系动词 is + 形容词补语 clear，not yet 表示否定和时间。
whether 引导的名词从句作真正主语，it 是形式主语。
连接两个并列分句。
条件副词从句，it is 后省略 possible。
指代前面 whether 从句的内容。
及物动词 require，将来时。
第一个宾语。
连接两个并列宾语。

chunks.text: a willingness to set aside self-interest
chunks.note: 第二个宾语，to set aside self-interest 是不定式短语作定语修饰 willingness。

grammar_points.name: 名词从句作主语
grammar_points.explain: 名词从句可以像名词一样在句中作主语。但英语不喜欢用长从句开头，所以常用 it 作形式主语，把真正的主语从句放到后面。比如 It is clear whether... 中，it 是形式主语，whether... 是真正主语。

grammar_points.name: 条件副词从句
grammar_points.explain: if 引导的从句表示条件，意思是“如果……”。注意 if 从句中常用一般现在时表示将来，主句用将来时。这里 if it is 是 if it is possible 的省略，it 指代前面 whether 从句的内容。

grammar_points.name: 不定式短语作定语
grammar_points.explain: 不定式 to do 可以放在名词后面作定语，表示“要做的事”。比如 a willingness to set aside self-interest 中，to set aside self-interest 修饰 willingness，说明是什么样的意愿。

aids.derivation: co-(共同) + oper(工作) + -ative(形容词后缀) → 共同工作的 → 合作的

synonyms.note: 更正式，常用于学术或工作场景
强调共同参与，如 joint effort

aids.derivation: will(意志) + -ing(形容词后缀) + -ness(名词后缀) → 愿意的状态 → 意愿

synonyms.note: 强调准备就绪的状态
更强调渴望，语气更强
更口语化，常见于日常对话
更正式，表示不理会

expressions.usage: 写作中可用于讨论团队合作、社会问题等：To achieve common goals, we must set aside self-interest.

sentences.en: Both general intelligence and moral reasoning are often thought to be uniquely human capacities.
sentences.zh: 通用智能和道德推理常常被认为是人类独有的能力。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + 主语并列
grammar.note: 主语为并列的 Both general intelligence and moral reasoning；谓语 are often thought to be uniquely human capacities，为被动结构 be thought to be。

pattern.label: S + V + C (主语 + 系动词 + 主语补语)

skeleton.text: Both general intelligence and moral reasoning

pattern.plain: 去掉修饰后，这句主干是：Both general intelligence and moral reasoning are thought to be capacities（通用智能和道德推理被认为是能力）。

chunks.text: Both general intelligence and moral reasoning
chunks.note: 并列主语，由 both...and... 连接。
被动语态，be thought to be 是固定结构，表示“被认为……”。
主语补语，说明主语是什么。

grammar_points.name: 被动语态
grammar_points.explain: 被动语态的结构是 be + 过去分词。这里 are thought 表示“被思考/被认为”，动作的发出者（人们）被省略了。雅思阅读中被动语态很常见，用来强调动作的承受者，而不是执行者。

grammar_points.name: S + V + C 句型
grammar_points.explain: 这个句型的动词是连缀动词（linking verb），比如 be、become、seem。这里 are thought to be 整体相当于一个连缀动词，后面接主语补语（uniquely human capacities）来说明主语是什么。

synonyms.note: 雅思阅读中可能用 general ability 替换 general intelligence
更正式的说法，雅思学术阅读中可能出现

aids.derivation: unique（独特的）+ -ly → 独特地

synonyms.note: 雅思阅读中替换 uniquely，表示“有区别地”

aids.derivation: cap（容纳）+ acity → 容纳的能力 → 能力

synonyms.note: 更常见的词，雅思阅读中常替换 capacities

expressions.usage: 写作中可用来表达普遍观点，例如：It is thought to be beneficial.（这被认为是有益的。）

sentences.en: But safety seems to require that we think of them as a package: if we are to give general intelligence to machines, we’ll need to give them moral authority, too.
sentences.zh: 但为了安全，我们似乎必须把它们视为一个整体：如果我们要赋予机器通用智能，就也得赋予它们道德权威。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + 冒号 + if 条件从句
grammar.note: 主干 But safety seems to require that we think of them as a package，that 引导宾语从句，think of A as B 表“把A视为B”；冒号后 if we are to give general intelligence to machines, we’ll need to give them moral authority，be to do 表将来计划。

pattern.label: S + Vt + O(主语 + 及物动词 + 宾语)

skeleton.text: But safety seems to require that we think of them as a package
skeleton.zh: 但安全似乎要求我们把它们视为一个整体

skeleton.text: that we think of them as a package
skeleton.zh: 我们把它们视为一个整体

pattern.plain: 去掉修饰后，主句主干是：safety seems to require that...（安全似乎要求……）。seem to require 是动词短语，后面跟一个宾语从句。

chunks.note: 转折连词，连接上一句。
主句的主语。
seem to do 表示“似乎……”，require 是及物动词，后接宾语从句。

chunks.text: that we think of them as a package
chunks.zh: 我们把它们视为一个整体
chunks.note: that 引导的宾语从句，作 require 的宾语。think of A as B 是固定搭配，意为“把A视为B”。
冒号引出解释说明的内容。

chunks.text: if we are to give general intelligence to machines
chunks.zh: 如果我们要赋予机器通用智能
chunks.note: if 引导的条件状语从句，be to do 表示按计划或意图要做的事。
逗号分隔从句和主句。

chunks.text: we’ll need to give them moral authority
chunks.zh: 我们就需要赋予它们道德权威
chunks.note: 主句，we'll need to give them moral authority，give 是双宾语动词，them 是间接宾语，moral authority 是直接宾语。
副词，表示“也”。

grammar_points.name: 名词从句（宾语从句）
grammar_points.explain: that 引导的从句可以当名词用，放在及物动词后面作宾语。这里 that we think of them as a package 整个就是 require 的宾语，说明“要求的内容是什么”。注意 that 在口语中常省略，但写作中保留更清晰。

grammar_points.name: 条件副词从句
grammar_points.explain: if 引导的从句表示“如果……”，交代主句成立的条件。这里 if we are to give... 中的 be to do 结构表示“打算/计划做某事”，比 will 更正式。条件句放在句首时，通常加逗号。

grammar_points.name: 句型四：S + V + O + O（双宾语）
grammar_points.explain: give 这类动词可以带两个宾语：一个给谁（间接宾语），一个给什么（直接宾语）。这里 give them moral authority，them 是间接宾语（机器），moral authority 是直接宾语（道德权威）。注意顺序：人（物）在前，物在后。

aids.derivation: re-（加强）+ quire（寻求）→ 一再寻求 → 要求

words.def: 获得（ac- 向 + quire → 寻求到）
询问（in- 进入 + quire → 深入寻求）

synonyms.note: 语气更强，常指强硬要求，雅思阅读中可替换 require
更口语化，表示需要

confusables.note: request 是“请求”，语气比 require 弱，require 更强调必要性

aids.derivation: pack（打包）+ -age（集合）→ 打包在一起的东西 → 一揽子

synonyms.note: 强调“捆在一起”，雅思阅读中常替换 package
更通用，表示一组事物

aids.derivation: moral（道德的）+ authority（权威）→ 道德上的权威

synonyms.note: 更学术，雅思阅读中可能替换 moral authority

expressions.usage: 写作中常用，如：We think of education as a right. 我们视教育为一项权利。
表示按计划或意图要做的事，比 will 更正式。如：The meeting is to start at 9. 会议计划9点开始。

sentences.en: And where exactly would that leave human beings? All the more reason to think about the destination now, and to be careful about what we wish for.
sentences.zh: 那么，这又究竟会把人类置于何地呢？这更加说明我们现在就应当思考那个终点，并谨慎对待自己所许下的愿望。

grammar.type: 特殊疑问句 + 省略句 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: 前句为特殊疑问句 where exactly would that leave human beings；后句 All the more reason to think about the destination now, and to be careful about what we wish for 为省略主谓的强调表达，两个不定式并列，what we wish for 为宾语从句。

questions.title: Questions 14–19
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 14–19 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt: What point does the writer make about AI in the first paragraph? A It is difficult to predict how quickly AI will progress. B Much can be learned about the use of AI in chess machines. C The future is unlikely to see limitations on the capabilities of AI. D Experts disagree on which specialised tasks AI will be able to perform.

pairs.q: unlikely to see limitations on the capabilities of AI
pairs.p: machines that can achieve human-level performance on the full range of tasks
pairs.note: 题干说‘未来不太可能看到AI能力的限制’，原文说‘机器能在所有任务上达到人类水平’，意思一致，都是说AI能力没有限制。
‘未来’对应‘到本世纪中叶’，都是指将来。

paraphrase.trap: A选项‘难以预测AI进展速度’原文未提及；B选项‘从象棋机器中学到很多’原文未提；D选项‘专家对AI能执行哪些专业任务有分歧’原文未提。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词‘AI’和‘first paragraph’以及‘unlikely to see limitations’，到原文找对应，发现‘achieve human-level performance on the full range of tasks’表达了没有限制的意思，故选C。

items.prompt: What is the writer doing in the second paragraph? A explaining why machines will be able to outperform humans B describing the characteristics that humans and machines share C giving information about the development of machine intelligence D indicating which aspects of humans are the most advanced

pairs.q: machines will be able to outperform humans
pairs.p: Machines will be free of many of the physical constraints on human intelligence
pairs.note: 题干说机器能超越人类，原文说机器摆脱人类智能的物理限制，意思一致。

paraphrase.trap: B选项说描述人类和机器共享的特征，但原文强调机器摆脱限制，并非共享。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'outperform humans'，到原文找表达机器超越人类能力的句子，发现'free of physical constraints'暗示机器不受限，从而能超越。

items.prompt: Why does the writer mention the story of King Midas? A to compare different visions of progress B to illustrate that poorly defined objectives can go wrong C to emphasise the need for cooperation D to point out the financial advantages of a course of action

pairs.note: 题干中'poorly defined objectives'对应原文'ask for the wrong thing'，都是指目标设定错误。
题干中'go wrong'对应原文'disastrous consequences'，表示糟糕的结果。
题干'poorly defined objectives'（定义不清的目标）与原文'ask for the wrong thing'（要错了东西）同义。

paraphrase.trap: A选项'compare different visions of progress'（比较不同的进步愿景）在原文未提及；C选项'need for cooperation'（合作需求）无对应；D选项'financial advantages'（经济优势）与原文'disastrous consequences'相反。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'poorly defined objectives'和'go wrong'，到原文找表达类似含义的短语，如'ask for the wrong thing'和'disastrous consequences'，从而定位到迈达斯故事的作用是举例说明目标错误导致恶果。

items.prompt: What challenge does the writer refer to in the fourth paragraph? A encouraging humans to behave in a more principled way B deciding which values we want AI to share with us C creating a better world for all creatures on the planet D ensuring AI is more human-friendly than we are ourselves

pairs.note: 题干中的challenge对应原文的problem，都是指难题。

pairs.q: deciding which values we want AI to share with us
pairs.p: tell the machines what they’re looking for
pairs.note: 题干说决定AI共享什么价值观，原文说告诉机器要找什么，意思一致。

paraphrase.trap: A选项‘鼓励人类更讲原则’和D选项‘确保AI比人类更友好’在原文未提及；C选项‘为所有生物创造更好世界’过于宽泛。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词‘challenge’和‘values’，到原文找对应词‘problem’和‘tell the machines what they’re looking for’，从而定位答案。

items.prompt: What does the writer suggest about the future of AI in the fifth paragraph? A The safety of machines will become a key issue. B It is hard to know what impact machines will have on the world. C Machines will be superior to humans in certain respects. D Many humans will oppose machines having a wider role.

pairs.q: machines will be superior to humans in certain respects
pairs.p: they’ll be better than us at finding them
pairs.note: 题干说机器在某些方面优于人类，原文说它们比我们更擅长找到路径，意思一致。

paraphrase.trap: A选项提到安全成为关键问题，但原文未涉及；B选项说难以知道机器的影响，原文明确说机器会更好；D选项说人类反对，原文未提。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'superior to humans'，到原文找'better than us'，即可定位答案。

items.prompt: Which of the following best summarises the writer’s argument in the sixth paragraph? A More intelligent machines will result in greater abuses of power. B Machine learning will share very few features with human learning. C There are a limited number of people with the knowledge to program machines. D Human shortcomings will make creating the machines we need more difficult.

questions.title: Questions 20–23
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 2?
In boxes 20–23 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

items.prompt: Machines with the ability to make moral decisions may prevent us from promoting the interests of our communities.

pairs.q: prevent us from promoting the interests of our communities
pairs.p: lose our freedom to discriminate in favour of our own communities
pairs.note: 题干说‘阻止我们促进社区利益’，原文说‘失去偏袒自己社区的自由’，意思一致，只是表达方式不同。

pairs.q: machines with the ability to make moral decisions
pairs.note: 题干主语是‘有道德决策能力的机器’，原文主语是‘我们’，但结合上下文，这里的‘我们’指代的是在机器影响下的人类，所以是隐含的替换。

paraphrase.trap: 题干中的‘prevent’可能被误解为‘阻止’，而原文用的是‘lose freedom’，但两者都表示失去某种能力或权利，所以是同意。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词‘prevent...promoting interests of communities’，到原文找类似表达，发现‘lose freedom to discriminate in favour of our own communities’与之对应，因此判断为YES。

items.prompt: Silicon police would need to exist in large numbers in order to be effective.

pairs.note: 题干和原文都出现了'silicon police'，这是定位的关键词。

pairs.q: would need to exist in large numbers in order to be effective
pairs.note: 题干说'需要大量存在才能有效'，原文问'我们准备好了吗'，两者讨论点不同，题干未提及数量或有效性的条件。

paraphrase.trap: 题干中的'large numbers'和'effective'在原文中没有对应信息，容易误判为False。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'Silicon police'定位到原文，但原文只问是否准备好，没有提到数量或有效性的条件，因此信息未给出。

items.prompt: Many people are comfortable with the prospect of their independence being restricted by machines.

pairs.q: comfortable with the prospect of their independence being restricted by machines
pairs.note: 题干说'舒适'，原文说'很少人欢迎'，即不舒适，反向表达。

paraphrase.trap: 题干中的'comfortable'可能被误认为与原文'welcome'同义，但实际是反向关系。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'comfortable'和'independence restricted'，到原文找对应情感态度的表达，发现'few of us are likely to welcome'表示否定，因此答案为NO。

items.prompt: If we want to ensure that machines act in our best interests, we all need to work together.

pairs.q: ensure that machines act in our best interests
pairs.note: 题干说'确保机器以我们最大利益行事'，原文说'搁置私利'，意思相近，都是为共同利益行动。

pairs.q: we all need to work together
pairs.note: 题干'一起工作'对应原文'合作精神'，同义替换。

paraphrase.trap: 题干中的'we all need to work together'可能被误认为需要所有人参与，但原文只说需要合作精神，未提所有人。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'work together'和'best interests'，到原文找'cooperative spirit'和'set aside self-interest'，发现意思一致，故答案为YES。

questions.title: Questions 24–26
instructions: Complete the summary using the list of phrases, A–F, below.
Write the correct letter, A–F, in boxes 24–26 on your answer sheet.
Using AI in the UK health system

items.prompt: AI currently has a limited role in the way 24 ____ are allocated in the health service.

pairs.note: limited role（有限作用）对应some input（一定参与），程度上的同义替换
allocated（分配）对应are used（被使用），动作的同义替换
health service（医疗服务）对应National Health Service (NHS)（国民医疗服务体系），具体化同义替换

paraphrase.trap: 题干中'currently'与原文'already'对应，但注意题干说'limited role'，原文是'some input'，程度略有不同，但属于同义替换。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'limited role'和'allocated'，到原文找对应表达'some input'和'are used'，同时'health service'对应'NHS'，即可定位答案。

items.prompt: However, such a change would result, for example, in certain 25 ____ not having their current level of 26 ____ .

pairs.note: 题干中的‘such a change’对应原文的‘we’d be depriving’，表示同一假设情况的结果。
题干‘certain’对应原文‘some’，‘25 ____’对应‘humans’，具体例子是‘senior doctors’。

pairs.q: not having their current level of 26 ____
pairs.p: of the control they presently enjoy
pairs.note: 题干‘not having’对应原文‘depriving...of’，‘current level of 26 ____’对应‘the control they presently enjoy’。

paraphrase.trap: 注意题干中‘such a change’可能误导考生寻找‘change’原词，但原文用‘depriving’表达变化结果。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词‘such a change’和‘not having...level of’，到原文找对应否定表达‘depriving...of’和‘control they presently enjoy’，从而定位答案。

items.prompt: ... certain medical practitioners not having their current level of 26 ____ .

pairs.q: not having their current level of
pairs.p: depriving some humans (e.g. senior doctors) of the control they presently enjoy
pairs.note: 题干说'没有当前水平的...'，原文说'剥夺...目前享有的控制'，意思相同，句式不同。
题干'某些医疗从业者'对应原文'资深医生'，是具体化替换。
题干'当前水平'对应原文'目前享有'，都是指现有的状态。

paraphrase.trap: 注意题干中'not having'是否定，原文'depriving...of'也是否定，但可能误以为找'have'的肯定表达。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'not having current level'和'medical practitioners'，到原文找否定表达和医生相关词，发现'depriving...of control'和'senior doctors'匹配。
